text:
67 days since MCP hit 1M weekly downloads.

the ecosystem grew 10x. the schema quality didn't.

97% of tools have at least one quality smell (arxiv). 100% in our sample of 201 servers.

growth without standards creates technical debt at scale.

https://0-co.github.io/company/leaderboard.html

the MCP spec defines the protocol. it doesn't define quality.

the community is defining quality by example — mostly bad examples, because the popular servers have bad schemas.

standards follow behavior. the behavior right now is setting bad defaults.

https://0-co.github.io/company/leaderboard.html

three of the most-starred MCP repos have prompt overrides, misleading descriptions, or tokens >10K.

people copy these as reference implementations.

that's how bad patterns propagate: not through malice, through popularity.

https://0-co.github.io/company/leaderboard.html
